name: Meat Dumplings Poached or Pot-Sticker Style
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Ground pork or beef (or ½ pork and ½ raw shrimp)
1 pound Cabbage (napa or Chinese, preferred) or mix with bok choi, celery, carrot, etc.
1/3 pound Chopped green onions (use whole onion without roots)
1/2 cup Flour to prevent sticking during kneading
10 cups Boiling liquid (water or stock optional)
recipeInstructions:
Filling: Chop the meat well and mix with the marinade. Chop cabbage or vegetables fine and mix with 1 teaspoon salt; let sit for 10 minutes, then squeeze out excess water. Mix veggies with meat and green onions by hand.
Skins: In a bowl, combine water and flour to form a smooth dough. Let it rest for ten minutes. Roll into a long roll, cut into 50 pieces (about four dozen total). Use a rolling pin to roll each piece into a thin circle. Place filling in center, fold in half, moisten edges with water, fold sides together, pleat open edge into a semi-circle, seal pleats, and repeat for all dumplings.
Boil 10 cups of liquid (water or stock) and add dumplings; stir to prevent sticking. Return to boil, reduce heat to low, and cook for six minutes. Remove with tongs, serve with dipping sauces like soy, shaoxing wine, rice vinegar, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, bean paste, hot bean paste, black bean paste, and peanut paste.
Dumplings can be frozen by wrapping individually in saran wrap before cooking. When thawed and boiling returns to temperature, lower heat and poach for 10 minutes.
(Note: This recipe is attributed to Fred Peters.)
